name: Taxonomy Mapping review for Immune Rejuvenation bundle
summary: The staged taxonomy mapping is acceptable. The outlook and intervention records explicitly separate immune rejuvenation from rapalogs/mTOR, autophagy/spermidine, microbiome/synbiotic, thymic/endocrine TRIIM, telomerase-adjacent, and vaccine-product efficacy claims while still allowing the immune track to cite shared boundary evidence.

findings.claim_or_issue: The support map spans mTOR/TORC1, spermidine/autophagy, TRIIM/thymic-endocrine, GIANTS-1 microbiome/immune, and review-level vaccine-response boundary evidence.
findings.why_it_matters: Without explicit branch boundaries, a single adjacent mechanism could be misread as support for the whole immune-rejuvenation track.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; retain branch-specific support-map labels, source IDs, finding IDs, and limitations during publication.
